name: Swordfish with Grapefruit and Rosemary Butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the swordfish steaks with salt and pepper.
2. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a heavy medium skillet over medium heat.
3. Add the fish to the skillet and cook until it is just opaque, about 9 minutes per inch of thickness, turning once.
4. Transfer the fish to a warm platter and tent with foil to keep warm.
5. Pour off all but a thin film of butter from the skillet.
6. Add shallots and rosemary and stir over medium heat until the shallots soften, about 2 minutes.
7. Add grapefruit juice. Increase the heat and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits.
8. Add juices from the fish platter.
9. Boil until the sauce is syrupy, 5 to 8 minutes.
10. Remove from heat and swirl in 2 tablespoons butter, 1 tablespoon at a time.
11. Season the sauce with salt and pepper.
12. Spoon the sauce over the swordfish.
13. Garnish with parsley.
